hero.title: Dissidia Duellum Final Fantasy Wiki
hero.description: A fast 3v3 boss-race action game set in modern Tokyo, where iconic Final Fantasy warriors build Bravery, unleash abilities, and defeat the boss before the rival team.

gameFeature.title: DISSIDIA DUELLUM FINAL FANTASY Official Launch Trailer
gameFeature.description: See the 3v3 Team Boss Battle Arena loop: purify crystals, build Bravery, Burst, and finish the boss.

releaseEditions.title: dissidiaduellumfinalfantasy beginner guide
releaseEditions.subtitle: Win your first sessions with a clean crystal-to-boss game plan

editions.name: Step 1: Crystal Priority
editions.releaseDate: Open every match with map control
includes: Clear monsters around crystal spawn sites before forced duels.
Secure purify timing to front-load team Bravery.
Rotate early when enemy pressure shifts to the next crystal.

editions.name: Step 2: Build Bravery
editions.releaseDate: Convert crystal control into Burst readiness
includes: Keep farming neutral objectives while denying free enemy stacks.
Use safe skirmishes only when they protect your crystal tempo.
Track teammate gauges and align your next push.

editions.name: Step 3: Burst Timing
includes: Trigger Bravery Burst with at least one ally in range.
Avoid solo Burst unless it creates guaranteed boss pressure.
Chain unique abilities inside your burst window.

editions.name: Step 4: Boss Burn
editions.releaseDate: Finish before the rival team stabilizes
includes: Dump stored cooldowns during boss vulnerability windows.
Keep one player peeling to stop enemy re-entry.
Reset quickly to crystal control after each damage phase.

highlights: Cloud, Warrior of Light, Terra, and Lightning are clean first mains.
Casual Battles are ideal for testing loadouts before ranked.
The crystal loop is the fastest path to consistent wins.

roster.title: dissidiaduellumfinalfantasy tier list
roster.subtitle: Rankings by objective, not a single flat ladder

ratings.title: dissidiaduellumfinalfantasy characters
ratings.subtitle: Launch roster snapshot with role identity and game impact

menTopRated.name: Cloud Strife - Melee carry with boss-damage passive
Warrior of Light - Team shield and stable frontline
Terra Branford - Safe ranged pressure and peel
Lightning - Fast rotations and monster clear tempo
Kain Highwind - Engage pressure via Jump access
Krile Mayer Baldesion - Crystal Bravery amplifier
Zidane Tribal - Bravery theft disruption specialist
Rinoa Heartilly - AoE control and comeback scaling
Gaia - Action-speed bruiser with debuff pressure

womenTopRated.name: Support Role Priority: Warrior of Light / Krile

highlights: Use role-specific rankings when drafting your trio.
Boss race value and crystal tempo both matter in final placement.
Tier shifts follow season rewards and balance patches.

controls.title: dissidiaduellumfinalfantasy reroll guide
controls.subtitle: Build a first-week account that matches your planned main

controls.input: Keep only if ability fits starter
Save when carry path is clear
Do not split upgrades too early

tips: A strong synergy start beats random high-rarity pulls.
Use Casual Battles to validate your reroll result.
Center upgrades on one carry before expanding your pool.

matchTypes.title: Best Team Comps for Launch
matchTypes.subtitle: Build around crystal control, Burst timing, and boss damage.
matchTypes.intro: Winning teams do three things well: build Bravery fast, enter Burst on time, and convert that window into boss damage. These launch-week comps use the official launch roster and role kits.

cards.title: Boss Rush Core
cards.whyItWorks: Cloud boosts boss damage, Warrior of Light protects the group and buffs nearby allies, and Krile increases team Bravery gain at crystals while passing Bravery to teammates.
cards.openingPlan: Stay grouped around early crystal fights, let Krile speed up Bravery income, and keep Cloud healthy for the first major Burst window.
cards.bestFor: Players who want a straightforward lineup for races against the boss.

cards.title: Crystal Control Core
cards.whyItWorks: Lightning clears monsters quickly, Krile boosts Bravery gained from purification, and Terra controls space from range while launching enemies away from key lanes.
cards.openingPlan: Rotate aggressively between crystal camps, clear mobs with Lightning, and let Terra cover approaches while Krile keeps the team topped up on Bravery.
cards.bestFor: Players who want to win through map tempo before the boss race fully starts.

cards.title: Pick and Collapse
cards.whyItWorks: Zidane steals Bravery, Kain closes gaps and launches targets, and Gaia boosts her own action speed while pressuring enemy tempo.
cards.openingPlan: Look for isolated targets between crystal fights, force short skirmishes, and cash in stolen Bravery before the enemy team can regroup.
cards.bestFor: Players who like fast collapses and constant pressure on enemy rotations.

cards.title: Safe All-Rounder
cards.whyItWorks: Cloud handles frontline pressure and boss damage, Terra gives ranged control, and Warrior of Light smooths out mistakes with protection and ally attack support.
cards.openingPlan: Let Warrior of Light anchor the team, keep Terra on safer angles, and use Cloud to finish openings and lead Burst damage.
cards.bestFor: Players who want a stable lineup with clear jobs for every role.

showcase.title: Four Roles Explained
showcase.subtitle: Know where to stand, what to do, and which launch characters fit each job.
showcase.intro: Every fighter belongs to one of four roles: Melee, Ranged, Agile, or Support. The fastest way to improve is to play your lane correctly before chasing flashy fights.

roleCards.officialSummary: High in stats, particularly in offensive power. Lead the way and support the frontline.
roleCards.jobInMatch: Start fights, pressure the boss lane, and force enemies away from your damage dealers.
roleCards.positioning: Play near the frontline and threaten short to mid-range paths.
roleCards.quickTip: Melee players should create space first and cash out on Burst second.

roleCards.officialSummary: Longer range for normal attacks and abilities. Try to strike from the backlines.
roleCards.jobInMatch: Cover crystal approaches, poke safely, and support boss windows without overcommitting.
roleCards.positioning: Stay behind the frontline and use range to keep control of angles.
roleCards.quickTip: Ranged players win by staying active without getting caught first.

roleCards.officialSummary: Dash with their unique ability and wreak havoc on the battlefield with high mobility.
roleCards.jobInMatch: Rotate quickly, punish weak targets, steal tempo, and disrupt enemy farming routes.
roleCards.positioning: Move between side lanes, crystal camps, and flanks instead of holding one spot.
roleCards.quickTip: Agile players should enter, disrupt, and leave before the enemy lineup closes around them.

roleCards.officialSummary: Possesses tools to support allies. Leverage each character's unique characteristics to your advantage.
roleCards.jobInMatch: Boost the team, stabilize Bravery, and make Burst windows safer and more consistent.
roleCards.positioning: Stay close enough to influence the fight while avoiding isolated duels.
roleCards.quickTip: Support players should track the whole fight, not just the enemy in front of them.

mygm.title: Abilities and Loadouts
mygm.subtitle: Mix signature tools, general skills, and role strengths to shape your playstyle.
mygm.intro: Each character equips five special abilities. Some are tied to that fighter, while others come from a broader pool of general skills.
loadoutBasics: Equip a mix of character-specific tools and general abilities.
Use your role to decide whether your loadout should lean toward pressure, mobility, support, or boss damage.
Builds feel strongest when they match the flow of the map: crystal farm, Burst entry, then boss damage.

signatureAbilities.uniqueEffect: Hold: Decreases damage taken by all allies by 15%.

signatureAbilities.passiveEffect: Increases ATK when unique ability attack lands (15s).
signatureAbilities.uniqueEffect: Hold: Leap forward and launch nearby foes into the air.

signatureAbilities.passiveEffect: Increases Bravery gained by the team when purifying a crystal.
signatureAbilities.uniqueEffect: Hold: Continuously give your Bravery to allies.

signatureAbilities.passiveEffect: Decreases cooldown time of fire abilities.
signatureAbilities.uniqueEffect: Hold: Launch nearby enemies into the air.
Hold: Charge a great distance forward, then unleash repeated attacks that launch foes into the air.

signatureAbilities.passiveEffect: Increases Bravery bonus when you respawn.
Increases movement SPD when HP is at 40% or below.
signatureAbilities.uniqueEffect: Hold: Dash and steal Bravery from foes in a cone-shaped area in front of you.

signatureAbilities.passiveEffect: Increases damage dealt to monsters (excluding bosses).
signatureAbilities.uniqueEffect: Hold: Dash and repeatedly attack nearby enemies.

signatureAbilities.passiveEffect: Increase DEF while action SPD is increased.
Increases damage during a burst chain.
signatureAbilities.uniqueEffect: Hold: Continuously attack enemies in front of you.

myrise.title: How to Win the Boss Race
myrise.subtitle: Farm Bravery, trigger Burst, and convert the window before the other team does.
myrise.intro: This game is not won by dueling forever. The real objective is to enter Burst on time and break the boss faster than the enemy team.

battleFlow.title: Purify crystals
battleFlow.description: Defeat monsters near a crystal to purify it and gain a large amount of team Bravery.

battleFlow.title: Reach max Bravery
battleFlow.description: At 9999 Bravery, your team can trigger Bravery Burst.

battleFlow.title: Enter Burst
battleFlow.description: Burst is your damage window against the boss.

battleFlow.title: Chain abilities into boss damage
battleFlow.description: Use special moves back to back to maintain pressure and push the boss down quickly.

battleFlow.title: Deny the enemy race
battleFlow.description: Defeating enemy players steals their Bravery and slows their next Burst.

coreRules: The boss is the win condition.
Burst mode is the key damage phase.
Enemy takedowns are valuable because they swing Bravery in your favor.

bestLaunchHelpers.reason: His passive increases damage dealt to bosses.
His passive increases damage during a burst chain.
She increases team Bravery gain at crystals and can feed Bravery to allies.
He protects the team during critical Burst setups.

doList: Farm early Bravery with purpose instead of taking random fights.
Enter Burst with teammates close enough to convert the window.
Use launch, protection, and mobility tools to keep the boss phase clean.
Turn enemy picks into immediate boss pressure whenever possible.
dontList: Do not chase low-value duels while your team loses crystal control.
Do not enter Burst alone if your team cannot follow up.
Do not ignore enemy Bravery totals during the race.
Do not waste your best movement tools before the boss window opens.

universeMode.title: Dissidia Duellum Final Fantasy Crystal Guide
universeMode.intro: Every match revolves around one core loop: purify crystals, build Bravery, unleash Bravery Burst, and race to defeat the boss before the enemy team.

quickFacts.label: Match Goal
quickFacts.value: Defeat the boss faster than the opposing team

quickFacts.label: Best Team Trick
quickFacts.value: A Burst Chain triggers when you and an ally unleash Bravery Burst on the boss at the same time

flowSteps.title: Purify crystals
flowSteps.body: Fight monsters near a crystal to purify it and gain a large amount of Bravery.

flowSteps.title: Reach maximum Bravery
flowSteps.body: Keep farming and rotating until your Bravery is full.

flowSteps.title: Trigger Bravery Burst
flowSteps.body: Unleash your power at maximum Bravery to open a damage window on the boss.

flowSteps.title: Finish the boss first
flowSteps.body: Use abilities and team timing to burn down the boss before the enemy side does.

tipCards.title: Crystals are the engine
tipCards.body: Crystal purification is the fastest way to build the Bravery you need for a boss push.

tipCards.title: Burst timing matters
tipCards.body: Bravery Burst is strongest when your team coordinates it instead of spending it one by one.

tipCards.title: Boss damage wins games
tipCards.body: Skirmishes matter, but the real finish line is the boss.

callouts.label: What to watch
callouts.text: Track when your team is close to max Bravery so you can convert a small lead into a full boss window.

callouts.label: Comeback pattern
callouts.text: A well-timed Burst Chain can quickly swing boss damage back in your favor.

callouts.label: New player focus
callouts.text: Learn the resource loop first, then worry about perfect duels.

communityCreations.title: Dissidia Duellum Final Fantasy Game Modes
communityCreations.eyebrow: Pick the right way to play
communityCreations.intro: DDFF supports competitive, casual, and solo-friendly play. The main modes are built around climbing, practicing, and collecting rewards in different ways.

modeCards.name: Season Rank Battles
modeCards.summary: Fight for ranking points during the active season and earn the highest Season Point payouts.
highlights: Winning and losing affect rank points
Still the best source of Season Points

modeCards.name: Casual Battles
modeCards.summary: Play multiplayer without changing your rank and test characters in a lower-pressure environment.
highlights: You can still earn sealed crystals

modeCards.name: Challenge Battles
modeCards.summary: Take on character-focused solo missions that double as a relaxed training mode.
highlights: Repeat clears can earn Season Points and sealed crystals

modeCards.name: Custom Matches
modeCards.summary: Set up your own battle room and choose the stage, enemies, and match parameters.

bestFor.reason: You can learn the pace of matches without risking rank.
They are built to help you practice one character at a time.
This is the core competitive ladder and the strongest Season Point source.
You get more control over the room setup and conditions.

theIsland.title: Dissidia Duellum Final Fantasy Season Rewards
theIsland.eyebrow: Progress through the monthly season loop
theIsland.intro: Seasons run for about one month. Play battles, earn Season Points, and use them to claim rewards that expand your roster, story, and account progression.

quickFacts.label: How to Earn Points
quickFacts.value: Participate in battles across the season

howItWorks.title: Join battles
howItWorks.body: Play through the season in modes tied to the active reward cycle.

howItWorks.title: Earn Season Points
howItWorks.body: Points are awarded through battle participation, with Season Rank Battles paying the most.

howItWorks.title: Claim rewards
howItWorks.body: Use your points to unlock rewards throughout the season.

howItWorks.title: Expand your account
howItWorks.body: Season rewards feed your story progress, upgrades, and long-term roster growth.

rewardCards.title: Main Stories
rewardCards.body: Season progress can unlock main story content tied to the current reward cycle.

rewardCards.title: Short Stories
rewardCards.body: Extra story scenes and side story content are part of the season reward lineup.

rewardCards.title: Characters
rewardCards.body: Seasons can include character unlocks as part of their reward structure.

rewardCards.title: Upgrade Materials
rewardCards.body: Enhancement materials are part of season progression and help strengthen your account.

rewardCards.title: Various Items
rewardCards.body: Additional items round out the season track and support regular play.

importantNotes.title: Play your way
importantNotes.body: Season progress is not locked to ranked wins only.

importantNotes.title: Rank still matters
importantNotes.body: Season Rank Battles remain the most efficient way to push your Season Point total.

importantNotes.title: Story is part of progression
importantNotes.body: Story content is tied directly into the season reward loop.

myfaction.title: Dissidia Duellum Final Fantasy Story Guide
myfaction.eyebrow: A new DISSIDIA story in modern Tokyo
myfaction.intro: A giant crystal appears in Tokyo and becomes part of everyday life. When strange monsters begin to emerge from the crystal's corruption, mysterious warriors answer the city's cry for help.

quickFacts.label: Central Threat
quickFacts.value: Monsters born from a polluted crystal

quickFacts.label: Story Presentation
quickFacts.value: Full Japanese voice acting and chat messenger episodes

storyBeats.title: A crystal enters daily life
storyBeats.body: A blue-glowing crystal appears in the city and soon becomes a familiar part of everyday Tokyo.

storyBeats.title: Peace suddenly breaks
storyBeats.body: The crystal is polluted by a mysterious force, and monsters begin to appear across the city.

storyBeats.title: The Ghosts arrive
storyBeats.body: Armed warriors emerge in response to the crisis, fight, and vanish before anyone can truly identify them.

storyBeats.title: Tokyo becomes the new stage
storyBeats.body: FINAL FANTASY heroes now live, fight, and form new bonds in a modern urban setting.

storyFeatures.title: Messenger Episodes
storyFeatures.body: See the characters' daily lives and interactions outside of battle through in-game chat scenes.

storyFeatures.title: Full Voice Acting
storyFeatures.body: The main presentation is delivered with full Japanese voice acting.

storyFeatures.title: Cel-Shaded Style
storyFeatures.body: The game presents its new world with stylized cel-shaded visuals.

lockerCodes.title: Dissidia Duellum Final Fantasy Challenges Guide
lockerCodes.intro: Challenge Battles are the best place to learn a character at your own pace while still earning useful rewards and progressing through the season.

quickFacts.label: Season Progress
quickFacts.value: Season Points are earned in any mode

sections.title: Why This Mode Matters
items: Challenge Battles are a relaxed mode designed to help you practice any character you have unlocked.
They are ideal for learning a new fighter before entering multiplayer.
Auto-play is available here, making it easier to study character flow and basic timing.

sections.title: What You Earn
items: Challenge Battles offer solo mission rewards.
Rewards include background music from the original FINAL FANTASY titles and various in-game items.
Season Points are still rewarded here, so challenge play contributes to seasonal progression.

sections.title: Best Times to Use Challenge Battles
items: Right after unlocking a new character
Before moving into Ranked or Casual Battles
When you want steady rewards without team matchmaking pressure

cta.label: Pick a Character and Start Practicing
cta.hint: Use Challenge Battles to learn controls, spacing, and ability timing before multiplayer.

pcRequirements.title: Dissidia Duellum Final Fantasy Controls Guide
pcRequirements.intro: DDFF is built for fast mobile play, but it also gives players room to tune controls, practice in Training Mode, and switch to controller support.

quickFacts.label: Core Style
quickFacts.value: Simple controls with multiple control settings

quickFacts.label: Auto-Control
quickFacts.value: Manual input can be used during Auto-Control

sections.title: Touch Controls
items: The game is built around easy-to-learn controls for mobile play.
You can switch between different control settings and choose the setup that feels best.
Training Mode lets you freely practice movement and character control.

sections.title: Controller Play
items: Controller support is available.
This gives players another option for movement, camera control, and ability comfort.
The official support note says controller support is included, though compatibility can vary by device.

sections.title: Semi-Auto Play
items: Auto-Control has been improved from the beta version.
You can step in with manual inputs while Auto-Control is active.
This creates a semi-auto playstyle that is useful for practice and lower-pressure content.

tips: Start in Training Mode to get used to your camera and movement rhythm.
Try a few control layouts before locking in your main setup.
Use semi-auto in easier content first, then switch to full manual for PvP improvement.

arenas.title: Dissidia Duellum Final Fantasy Customization Guide
arenas.eyebrow: Style, Stats, Music, and Social Features
arenas.intro: Customization in DDFF is more than cosmetics. You can change outfits, equip Memorias for stat boosts, collect music, tune support setups, and personalize your profile.

quickFacts.label: Social Features
quickFacts.value: Profile editing, chat, and FF tags

sections.title: Outfits
items: Characters can wear their original looks or new outfits adapted to modern Tokyo.
This lets players build a roster that matches both classic FF nostalgia and the new DDFF setting.

sections.title: Memorias
items: Memorias feature memorable scenes from across the FINAL FANTASY series.
They can be earned as rewards.
You can set Memorias to boost your character stats.

sections.title: BGM Collection
items: You can obtain music tracks from across the FINAL FANTASY series and arranged versions.
Collected BGM can be used to elevate battles.
The launch celebration login bonus includes 8 BGM tracks from past DISSIDIA titles.

sections.title: Profile, Chat, and Support Tools
items: You can edit your profile and chat with other players.
The game includes tags for talking about your favorite FINAL FANTASY titles.
Support Sets can be auto-created based on the character-boosting stats you want to prioritize.

showcaseCards.title: Original & Modern Outfits
showcaseCards.body: Swap between classic looks and modern-world styles.

showcaseCards.title: Memorias
showcaseCards.body: Collect iconic FF scenes and use them for stat growth.

showcaseCards.title: Battle Music
showcaseCards.body: Unlock original and arranged tracks from across the series.

showcaseCards.title: Player Identity
showcaseCards.body: Build your profile, use tags, and connect with the community.

dlcUnlockables.title: Dissidia Duellum Final Fantasy Roadmap
dlcUnlockables.eyebrow: Launch Content, Seasons, and Upcoming Characters
dlcUnlockables.intro: DISSIDIA DUELLUM FINAL FANTASY is live now on iOS and Android, with a launch roster of 10 characters and a two-month post-launch campaign adding 6 more fighters.

seasonInfo.title: Season Structure
items: Seasons run for about one month.
Players can earn Season Points through battles.
Season rewards include main stories, short stories, characters, and various items.

characters.name: Onion Knight
characters.summary: A late-bloomer fighter who grows stronger as the battle develops.

characters.name: Iroha
characters.summary: Builds power by chaining abilities in succession.

characters.name: Firion
characters.summary: Uses the Blood Sword to recover HP while dealing damage.

characters.name: Balthier
characters.summary: A PvP-focused gunner who gains an edge after defeating enemy players.

characters.name: Rikku
characters.summary: Uses Mix-inspired abilities to create a wide range of effects.

characters.name: Clive Rosfield
characters.summary: A well-rounded fighter with powerful semi-Primed attacks.

liveNow.title: Current Login Bonus
items: 1,000 MogPay Points on Day 1
Draw Ticket x10 on Day 2
5,000 Growth Eggs on Day 3
1,000 MogPay Points on Day 4
1,000 MogPay Points on Day 5
Draw Ticket x10 on Day 6
Character Ticket x5 on Day 7

editionPacks.name: Upcoming Characters
includes: Track wave windows and role additions.
Set team plans around incoming role coverage.
Keep resources ready for debut character testing.

editionPacks.name: Patch Note Checklist
includes: Review season point and reward changes.
Adjust your mode rotation for monthly goals.
Re-check ranking strategy after each content wave.

editionPacks.name: Event Reward Plan
includes: Target login rewards during active windows.
Convert tickets and points into roster growth.
Plan around campaign deadlines in UTC.

editionPacks.name: Competitive Prep
includes: Keep one stable ranked trio for climb days.
Use Challenge Battles to prep incoming characters.
Apply control tuning before high-pressure sessions.

faq.title: dissidiaduellumfinalfantasy
faq.subtitle: Fast answers for the core gameplay loop and progression path

questions.question: How do I win faster in dissidiaduellumfinalfantasy?
questions.answer: In dissidiaduellumfinalfantasy, prioritize crystals, build Bravery, coordinate Burst timing, and convert that window into boss damage.

questions.question: Which starter is best in dissidiaduellumfinalfantasy?
questions.answer: For dissidiaduellumfinalfantasy, Cloud is the easiest boss-pressure start, Warrior of Light is the safest team option, and Terra is the clean ranged learner.

questions.question: Does dissidiaduellumfinalfantasy reroll matter?
questions.answer: Yes. dissidiaduellumfinalfantasy reroll quality matters because role-aligned early pulls create a much smoother first-week progression path.

questions.question: What should I train first in dissidiaduellumfinalfantasy?
questions.answer: Start dissidiaduellumfinalfantasy training with crystal rotation timing, burst-call coordination, and one stable three-player lineup.

questions.question: How often should I check the dissidiaduellumfinalfantasy roadmap?
questions.answer: Check the dissidiaduellumfinalfantasy roadmap at least once per week so you can pre-farm for incoming rewards and character updates.

questions.question: Where can I follow official dissidiaduellumfinalfantasy updates?
questions.answer: Follow official dissidiaduellumfinalfantasy updates via the Square Enix site, DDFF EN X account, and the App Store/Google Play pages.

cta.title: Ready for dissidiaduellumfinalfantasy?
cta.description: Use the module hub, lock your starter plan, and run your first coordinated boss race.
